title: LATAM Health Literacy & Education Gap
problem: Health literacy in LATAM is low, especially in lower socioeconomic groups. Patients don't understand their conditions, medications, or treatment plans. This leads to poor adherence, preventable complications, and unnecessary ER visits. No culturally-appropriate patient education materials at scale. Doctors don't have time to explain fully.
solution: Health education platform with culturally-adapted content. Video explainers for common conditions (diabetes, hypertension, pregnancy). Medication information sheets. Procedure preparation materials. Multilingual (Spanish, Portuguese, indigenous languages). Integration with EMR to auto-recommend content. Patient comprehension tracking.
technology: Video content library, mobile app, EMR integration, content recommendation engine, comprehension quizzes, multilingual TTS
target_customer: Hospital systems, health insurers (reduce complications), pharmaceutical companies (medication education), Ministry of Health
ai_type: Content recommendation based on patient profile
traction_signals: Low health literacy documented across LATAM
Proven models (K Health, Healthwise in US)
regulatory_notes: Medical accuracy requirements, patient data privacy
varden_fit: MEDIUM FIT - Content-heavy but has infrastructure component (delivery platform, EMR integration). Could license to insurers and hospital systems.
notes: Health education is unglamorous but impactful. Culturally-appropriate content is key. Partner with medical societies for credibility.
